title: Mustardy Chinese Cheonggukjang Pancakes
description: A savory and indulgent Hunan dish featuring cheonggukjang and confit-tomato, made with a low-glycemic-friendly approach, cooked to perfection on a tava-griddle.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together cheonggukjang, confit-tomato, mustard, scallions, eggs, almond flour, salt, and black pepper until well combined.
Heat a tava-griddle over medium heat and brush with coconut oil.
Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup, scoop the cheonggukjang mixture onto the tava-griddle.
Flatten the mixture slightly with a spatula and cook for 3-4 minutes or until the bottom is golden brown.
Loosen the pancake with a spatula and flip it over, cooking for an additional 3-4 minutes or until the other side is also golden brown.
Repeat the process with the remaining mixture, adjusting the heat as needed to prevent burning.
Serve the Mustardy Chinese Cheonggukjang Pancakes hot, garnished with additional scallions if desired.
